headline: Three new labs proposed. Pickleball sponsorship goldmine found.
narrative:
Your labs found some real money today. The pickle-idea lab discovered four equipment categories where zero brands are currently showing up — sponsorship whitespace worth exploring. They also built a player endorsement map with 179 player-brand pairs that appear together multiple times, which means you've got intel on who's actually influencable in this space. On the execution side, their shot classifier is crushing it at 99% precision, and they figured out why five badge types kept getting missed — they're adding positive examples to the prompt.

The general-purpose lab is getting creative, surfacing two wildcard ideas about AI scouting networks and viral clip factories, but here's the thing: the meta-lab flagged that this lab is 40% wildcard ideas with almost no execution risk assessment. You're getting big swings, but no filter for feasibility. That's not bad — it's just blind in one direction.

Most importantly, the meta-lab is recommending three new labs: one to infer missing metadata (which keeps showing up as a gap), one to stratify content by quality (because 27% of your footage is low-quality noise), and one to track individual players over time. These aren't random — they're patterns the system saw repeating across your existing labs. Worth spinning up at least the metadata lab this week.

callouts.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories, zero brand presence
callouts.why_matters: Direct revenue opportunity. If brands aren't showing up, you can own the first-mover advantage in pickleball equipment sponsorship.
callouts.action: Have sales reach out to equipment manufacturers in those 4 categories. You have data they don't.

callouts.title: Three new lab proposals from meta-lab
callouts.why_matters: These aren't random suggestions — they're gaps the system detected across multiple lab runs. Metadata-completeness alone could unlock a lot of downstream value.
callouts.action: Greenlight at least metadata-completeness-inference. The other two (quality stratification, player dynamics) are strong seconds.

callouts.title: General-purpose lab is 40% wildcard, 0% risk-filtered
callouts.why_matters: You're getting creative ideas (Moneyball AI Scout, Viral Clip Factory) but they're not vetted for feasibility. You need a quality gate.
callouts.action: Add an execution-risk assessor to that lab's personas, or route wildcards through a quick feasibility check before they bubble up.

callouts.title: Player longitudinal tracking is viable: 65 players with 5+ clips
callouts.why_matters: You can now build time-series analysis on player skill progression. This unlocks personalized content and training recommendations.
callouts.action: Flag this for the player-longitudinal-dynamics lab once it spins up. It's your richest dataset for coaching tools.

callouts.title: Shot classifier at 99% F1 — prompt fix boosted it further
callouts.why_matters: Your production pipeline just got more reliable. Fewer false positives means cleaner data downstream.
callouts.action: Just notice. The system is self-correcting and working.

pickle-idea-lab.top_title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
